titleStmt.title: SII 23.75: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 75. (A.R. No. 75 of 1907.) Ambāsamudram, Ambasamudram Taluk, Tirunelveli District Tirumūlanāthasvāmi temple—on the same wall Jat. Sundara-Chōḷa-Pāṇḍya: year 14

summary.p: This is an inscription of Jaṭāvarman Śundara-Chōḷa-Pāṇḍyadēva, who, it is learnt from No. 112 of 1905 found at Mannārkōyil near Ambāsamudram, was a son of Rājēndra-Chōḷa I. He was evidently appointed by the latter as his viceroy over the Pāṇḍya country after its reannexation to his kingdom (An. Rept. for 1905, para 25). According to that inscription the 15th year of Sundara-Chōḷa-Pāṇḍya corresponded to the 24th year of Rājēndra-Chōḷa (acc. A.D. 1012). Hence the date of the present record would be about A.D. 1036.

It registers a gift of a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tiruchchālattuṟai-uḍaiya-Bhaṭāra by a resident of the place by name Vāsudēva-Kramavittaṉ. Muḷḷi-nāḍu is here stated to have formed a division of Muḍigoṇḍaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu in Rājarājamaṇḍalam.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . koccaṭaiyavarmarāṉa śrī cun=taracoḷapāṇṭiya devarkku yāṇṭu patunālāvatu śrī rāja[rāja]maṇṭalattu mu
ṭikoṇṭa coḷavaḷanāṭṭu muḷḷināṭṭu bramadeyam śrīrājarājacatu rvetimaṅkalattu tiruccālattuṟai śrī ṉa Uṭai
yār bhaṭāraparamecurarkku Ivvūr śrī pañcavaṉmātevicceri Āli koṉṟai śrīvāsudevakiramavit ntāviḷa
tmakku
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 149
Digital edition of SII 23.75 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
